v.CNV: “各位父老弟兄，请听听我现在对你们的申辩。”
v.ESV: “Brothers and fathers, hear the defense that I now make before you.”
v.GAE: 부형들아 내가 지금 여러분 앞에서 변명하는 말을 들으라
v.KJV: Men, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence which I make now unto you.
v.NASB: 'Brethren and fathers, hear my defense which I now offer to you.'
v.NIV: “Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.”
v.NRSV: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense that I now make before you.”
v.SAENEW: "동포 여러분, 내가 이제 여러분에게 드리는 해명을 잘 들어 주시기 바랍니다."
v.WLB: “형제들과 어르신 되시는 동포 여러분, 이제 내가 해명하는 것을 잘 들어 주시기 바랍니다.”

v.CNV: 他们听见保罗用希伯来语说话，就更加安静了。保罗说：
v.ESV: And when they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew language, they became even more quiet. And he said:
v.GAE: 그들이 그가 히브리 말로 말함을 듣고 더욱 조용한지라 이어 이르되
v.KJV: (And when they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence: and he saith,)
v.NASB: And when they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew dialect, they became even more quiet; and he said,
v.NIV: When they heard him speak to them in Aramaic, they became very quiet. Then Paul said:
v.NRSV: When they heard him addressing them in Hebrew, they became even more quiet. Then he said:
v.SAENEW: 군중들은 바울이 히브리 말로 연설하는 것을 듣고, 더욱더 조용해졌다. 바울은 말을 이었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 바울이 히브리 언어로 말하는 것을 듣고는 이내 조용해졌습니다. 그러자 바울이 계속 말했습니다.

v.CNV: “我是犹太人，生在基利家的大数，在城里长大，按照我们祖宗律法的严格要求，在迦玛列门下受教，我为　神热心，好像你们大家今天一样。
v.ESV: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated at the feet of Gamaliel according to the strict manner of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God as all of you are this day.
v.GAE: 나는 유대인으로 길리기아 다소에서 났고 이 성에서 자라 가말리엘의 문하에서 우리 조상들의 율법의 엄한 교훈을 받았고 오늘 너희 모든 사람처럼 하나님께 대하여 열심이 있는 자라
v.KJV: I am verily a man which am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.
v.NASB: 'I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated under Gamaliel, strictly according to the law of our fathers, being zealous for God just as you all are today.
v.NIV: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city. I studied under Gamaliel and was thoroughly trained in the law of our ancestors. I was just as zealous for God as any of you are today.
v.NRSV: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, educated strictly according to our ancestral law, being zealous for God, just as all of you are today.
v.SAENEW: "나는 유대 사람입니다. 나는 길리기아의 다소에서 태어나서, 이 도시 예루살렘에서 자랐고, 가말리엘 선생의 문하에서 우리 조상의 율법의 엄격한 방식을 따라 교육을 받았습니다. 그래서 나는 오늘날 여러분 모두가 그러하신 것과 같이, 하나님께 열성적인 사람이었습니다.
v.WLB: “나는 길리기아 지방의 다소에서 태어난 유대 사람이지만 이 도시에서 자랐습니다. 나는 가말리엘의 지도 가운데 우리 조상들의 율법으로 엄격한 훈련을 받았고 오늘 여기 모인 여러분 못지않게 하나님께 대한 열심이 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 我曾经迫害信奉这道的人直至死地，把男男女女都捆绑起来，送进监狱，
v.ESV: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering to prison both men and women,
v.GAE: 내가 이 도를 박해하여 사람을 죽이기까지 하고 남녀를 결박하여 옥에 넘겼노니
v.KJV: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.
v.NASB: 'I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and putting both men and women into prisons,
v.NIV: I persecuted the followers of this Way to their death, arresting both men and women and throwing them into prison,
v.NRSV: I persecuted this Way up to the point of death by binding both men and women and putting them in prison,
v.SAENEW: 나는 이 '도'를 따르는 사람들을 박해하여 죽이기까지 하였고, 남자든 여자든 가리지 않고 묶어서 감옥에 넣었습니다.
v.WLB: 그래서 나는 이 도를 따르는 사람들을 죽이기까지 핍박하며 남녀를 가리지 않고 모두 잡아다가 감옥에 집어넣었습니다.

v.CNV: 这是大祭司和全公议会都可以给我作证的。我也从他们那里得到了写给众弟兄的信，就去大马士革，要把那里的人捆绑起来，带到耶路撒冷接受惩罚。
v.ESV: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can bear me witness. From them I received letters to the brothers, and I journeyed toward Damascus to take those also who were there and bring them in bonds to Jerusalem to be punished.
v.GAE: 이에 대제사장과 모든 장로들이 내 증인이라 또 내가 그들에게서 다메섹 형제들에게 가는 공문을 받아 가지고 거기 있는 자들도 결박하여 예루살렘으로 끌어다가 형벌 받게 하려고 가더니
v.KJV: As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.
v.NASB: as also the high priest and all the Council of the elders can testify. From them I also received letters to the brethren, and started off for Damascus in order to bring even those who were there to Jerusalem as prisoners to be punished.
v.NIV: as the high priest and all the Council can themselves testify. I even obtained letters from them to their associates in Damascus, and went there to bring these people as prisoners to Jerusalem to be punished.
v.NRSV: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. From them I also received letters to the brothers in Damascus, and I went there in order to bind those who were there and to bring them back to Jerusalem for punishment.
v.SAENEW: 내 말이 사실임을 대제사장과 모든 장로가 증언하실 것입니다. 나는 그들에게서 다마스쿠스에 있는 동포들에게 보내는 공문을 받아서, 다마스쿠스로 길을 떠났습니다. 나는 거기에 있는 신도들까지 잡아서 예루살렘으로 끌어다가, 처벌을 받게 하려고 했던 것입니다."
v.WLB: 그것은 대제사장과 모든 공회원들이 증언할 수 있을 것입니다. 나는 심지어 그들로부터 다메섹에 있는 형제들에게 보낼 공문을 얻어 냈고 그곳에 있는 신자들을 붙잡아 예루살렘으로 데려와 처벌받게 하려고 다메섹으로 떠났습니다.

v.CNV: “约在正午，当我走近大马士革的时候，忽然有大光从天上向我四面照射，
v.ESV: “As I was on my way and drew near to Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone around me.
v.GAE: 가는 중 다메섹에 가까이 갔을 때에 오정쯤 되어 홀연히 하늘로부터 큰 빛이 나를 둘러 비치매
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.
v.NASB: 'But it happened that as I was on my way, approaching Damascus about noontime, a very bright light suddenly flashed from heaven all around me,
v.NIV: “About noon as I came near Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven flashed around me.
v.NRSV: “While I was on my way and approaching Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone about me.
v.SAENEW: "가다가, 정오 때쯤에 다마스쿠스 가까이에 이르렀는데, 갑자기 하늘로부터 큰 빛이 나를 둘러 비추었습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 다메섹에 가까이 다다르자 정오쯤 됐는데 갑자기 하늘에서 밝은 빛이 내 주위를 둘러 비추었습니다.

v.CNV: 我仆倒在地上，听见有声音对我说：‘扫罗，扫罗，你为什么迫害我？’
v.ESV: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
v.GAE: 내가 땅에 엎드러져 들으니 소리 있어 이르되 사울아 사울아 네가 왜 나를 박해하느냐 하시거늘
v.KJV: And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me, Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?
v.NASB: and I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?'
v.NIV: I fell to the ground and heard a voice say to me, ‘Saul! Saul! Why do you persecute me?’
v.NRSV: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
v.SAENEW: 나는 땅바닥에 엎어졌는데 '사울아, 사울아, 네가 어찌하여 나를 핍박하느냐?' 하는 소리가 들려왔습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 땅에 풀썩 쓰러졌는데 한 음성이 내게 말씀하시는 것이 들렸습니다. ‘사울아! 사울아! 네가 왜 나를 핍박하느냐?’

v.CNV: 我回答：‘主啊，你是谁？’他说：‘我就是你所迫害的拿撒勒人耶稣。’
v.ESV: And I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ And he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.’
v.GAE: 내가 대답하되 주님 누구시니이까 하니 이르시되 나는 네가 박해하는 나사렛 예수라 하시더라
v.KJV: And I answered, Who art thou, Lord? And he said unto me, I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom thou persecutest.
v.NASB: 'And I answered, 'Who are You, Lord?' And He said to me, 'I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are persecuting.'
v.NIV: “‘Who are you, Lord?’ I asked. “ ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting,’ he replied.
v.NRSV: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ Then he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth whom you are persecuting.’
v.SAENEW: 그래서 내가 '주님, 누구십니까?' 하고 물었더니, 그는 나에게 대답하시기를 '나는 네가 핍박하는 나사렛 예수이다' 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 물었습니다. ‘주여, 당신은 누구십니까?’ 그분이 내게 대답하셨습니다. ‘나는 네가 핍박하는 나사렛 예수다.’

v.CNV: 跟我在一起的人，只看见那光，却听不清楚那位对我说话的声音。
v.ESV: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not understand the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
v.GAE: 나와 함께 있는 사람들이 빛은 보면서도 나에게 말씀하시는 이의 소리는 듣지 못하더라
v.KJV: And they that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
v.NASB: 'And those who were with me saw the light, to be sure, but did not understand the voice of the One who was speaking to me.
v.NIV: My companions saw the light, but they did not understand the voice of him who was speaking to me.
v.NRSV: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not hear the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
v.SAENEW: 나와 함께 있는 사람들은, 그 빛은 보았으나, 내게 말씀하시는 분의 음성은 듣지 못하였습니다.
v.WLB: 나와 함께 있는 사람들은 빛은 보았지만 내게 말씀하시는 분의 음성은 알아듣지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 我说：‘主啊，我应当作什么呢？’主说：‘起来，进大马士革去，在那里有人会把指定给你作的一切事告诉你。’
v.ESV: And I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ And the Lord said to me, ‘Rise, and go into Damascus, and there you will be told all that is appointed for you to do.’
v.GAE: 내가 이르되 주님 무엇을 하리이까 주께서 이르시되 일어나 다메섹으로 들어가라 네가 해야 할 모든 것을 거기서 누가 이르리라 하시거늘
v.KJV: And I said, What shall I do, Lord? And the Lord said unto me, Arise, and go into Damascus; and there it shall be told thee of all things which are appointed for thee to do.
v.NASB: 'And I said, 'What shall I do, Lord?' And the Lord said to me, 'Get up and go on into Damascus, and there you will be told of all that has been appointed for you to do.'
v.NIV: “‘What shall I do, Lord?’ I asked. “ ‘Get up,’ the Lord said, ‘and go into Damascus. There you will be told all that you have been assigned to do.’
v.NRSV: I asked, ‘What am I to do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go to Damascus; there you will be told everything that has been assigned to you to do.’
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 내가 '주님, 어떻게 하라 하십니까?' 하고 말하였더니, 주님께서 내게 말씀하셨습니다. '일어나서, 다마스쿠스로 가거라. 거기에는 네가 해야 할 모든 일을 누가 말해 줄 것이다.'
v.WLB: 내가 물었습니다. ‘주여, 내가 어떻게 해야 합니까?’ 주께서 말씀하셨습니다. ‘일어나 다메섹으로 들어가거라. 거기서 네가 할 일을 모두 일러 줄 것이다.’

v.CNV: 因为那光太强烈，我的眼睛就瞎了，跟我在一起的人就牵着我的手，进了大马士革。
v.ESV: And since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me, and came into Damascus.
v.GAE: 나는 그 빛의 광채로 말미암아 볼 수 없게 되었으므로 나와 함께 있는 사람들의 손에 끌려 다메섹에 들어갔노라
v.KJV: And when I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
v.NASB: 'But since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me and came into Damascus.
v.NIV: My companions led me by the hand into Damascus, because the brilliance of the light had blinded me.
v.NRSV: Since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, those who were with me took my hand and led me to Damascus.
v.SAENEW: 나는 그 빛의 광채 때문에 눈이 멀어서, 함께 가던 사람들의 손에 이끌려 다마스쿠스로 갔습니다.
v.WLB: 그 빛의 광채로 인해 내 눈이 멀게 돼 함께 있던 사람들이 내 손을 잡고 다메섹으로 데려다 주었습니다.

v.CNV: “有一个人名叫亚拿尼亚的，他是一个虔诚而遵守律法的人，当地所有的犹太人都称赞他。
v.ESV: “And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
v.GAE: 율법에 따라 경건한 사람으로 거기 사는 모든 유대인들에게 칭찬을 듣는 아나니아라 하는 이가
v.KJV: And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
v.NASB: 'A certain Ananias, a man who was devout by the standard of the Law, and well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
v.NIV: “A man named Ananias came to see me. He was a devout observer of the law and highly respected by all the Jews living there.
v.NRSV: “A certain Ananias, who was a devout man according to the law and well spoken of by all the Jews living there,
v.SAENEW: 거기에 아나니아라는 사람이 있었습니다. 그는 율법을 따라 사는 경건한 사람으로, 거기에 사는 모든 유대 사람에게 칭찬을 받는 사람이었습니다.
v.WLB: 그곳에 아나니아라는 사람이 살고 있었습니다. 그는 율법을 잘 지키는 경건한 사람으로 다메섹에 사는 모든 유대 사람들에게 깊은 존경을 받고 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 他来见我，站在我旁边，对我说：‘扫罗弟兄，你现在可以看见了。’我立刻往上一看，看见了他。
v.ESV: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight.’ And at that very hour I received my sight and saw him.
v.GAE: 내게 와 곁에 서서 말하되 형제 사울아 다시 보라 하거늘 즉시 그를 쳐다보았노라
v.KJV: Came unto me, and stood, and said unto me, Brother Saul, receive thy sight. And the same hour I looked up upon him.
v.NASB: came to me, and standing near said to me, 'Brother Saul, receive your sight!' And at that very time I looked up at him.
v.NIV: He stood beside me and said, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ And at that very moment I was able to see him.
v.NRSV: came to me; and standing beside me, he said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight!’ In that very hour I regained my sight and saw him.
v.SAENEW: 그가 나를 찾아와 곁에 서서, '형제 사울이여, 눈을 뜨시오' 하고 나에게 말하였습니다. 그 순간에 나는 시력을 회복하여, 그를 쳐다보았습니다.
v.WLB: 그가 나를 찾아와 내 곁에 서서 말했습니다. ‘사울 형제, 다시 눈을 뜨시오!’ 바로 그 순간 나는 눈을 떠 그를 볼 수 있게 됐습니다.

v.CNV: 他又说：‘我们祖先的　神选派了你，让你明白他的旨意，看见那义者，听见他口中的声音。
v.ESV: And he said, ‘The God of our fathers appointed you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear a voice from his mouth;
v.GAE: 그가 또 이르되 우리 조상들의 하나님이 너를 택하여 너로 하여금 자기 뜻을 알게 하시며 그 의인을 보게 하시고 그 입에서 나오는 음성을 듣게 하셨으니
v.KJV: And he said, The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
v.NASB: 'And he said, 'The God of our fathers has appointed you to know His will and to see the Righteous One and to hear an utterance from His mouth.
v.NIV: “Then he said: ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will and to see the Righteous One and to hear words from his mouth.
v.NRSV: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear his own voice;
v.SAENEW: 그때에 아나니아가 내게 말하였습니다. '우리 조상의 하나님께서 당신을 택하셔서, 자기의 뜻을 알게 하시고, 그 의로우신 분을 보게 하시고, 그분의 입에서 나오는 음성을 듣게 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 그러자 아나니아가 말했습니다. ‘우리 조상들의 하나님께서 당신을 선택해 그분의 뜻을 알게 하시고 의로우신 그분을 보게 하시고 그분의 입에서 나오는 음성을 듣게 하셨습니다.

v.CNV: 因为你要把所看见所听见的，向万人为他作见证。
v.ESV: for you will be a witness for him to everyone of what you have seen and heard.
v.GAE: 네가 그를 위하여 모든 사람 앞에서 네가 보고 들은 것에 증인이 되리라
v.KJV: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
v.NASB: 'For you will be a witness for Him to all men of what you have seen and heard.
v.NIV: You will be his witness to all people of what you have seen and heard.
v.NRSV: for you will be his witness to all the world of what you have seen and heard.
v.SAENEW: 당신은 그분을 위하여 모든 사람에게 당신이 보고 들은 것을 증언하는 증인이 될 것입니다.
v.WLB: 당신은 보고 들은 것을 모든 사람에게 전하는 예수의 증인이 될 것입니다.

v.CNV: 现在你为什么还耽搁呢？起来受洗，求告他的名，洗净你的罪吧。’
v.ESV: And now why do you wait? Rise and be baptized and wash away your sins, calling on his name.’
v.GAE: 이제는 왜 주저하느냐 일어나 주의 이름을 불러 세례를 받고 너의 죄를 씻으라 하더라
v.KJV: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
v.NASB: 'Now why do you delay? Get up and be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on His name.'
v.NIV: And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized and wash your sins away, calling on his name.’
v.NRSV: And now why do you delay? Get up, be baptized, and have your sins washed away, calling on his name.’
v.SAENEW: 그러니 이제 망설일 까닭이 어디 있습니까? 일어나, 주님의 이름을 불러서, 세례를 받고, 당신의 죄 씻음을 받으시오.'"
v.WLB: 그러니 이제 당신이 망설일 이유가 무엇입니까? 일어나서 세례를 받고 주의 이름을 불러 죄 씻음을 받으시오.’

v.CNV: “后来，我回到耶路撒冷，在殿里祷告的时候，魂游象外，
v.ESV: “When I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
v.GAE: 후에 내가 예루살렘으로 돌아와서 성전에서 기도할 때에 황홀한 중에
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
v.NASB: 'It happened when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I fell into a trance,
v.NIV: “When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying at the temple, I fell into a trance
v.NRSV: “After I had returned to Jerusalem and while I was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
v.SAENEW: "그 뒤에 내가 예루살렘으로 돌아와서, 성전에서 기도하는 가운데 황홀경에 빠져
v.WLB: 그 후 내가 예루살렘으로 돌아와 성전에서 기도하고 있을 때였습니다. 나는 환상에 빠져

v.CNV: 看见主对我说：‘你要快快离开耶路撒冷，因为你为我作的见证，他们是不会接受的。’
v.ESV: and saw him saying to me, ‘Make haste and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
v.GAE: 보매 주께서 내게 말씀하시되 속히 예루살렘에서 나가라 그들은 네가 내게 대하여 증언하는 말을 듣지 아니하리라 하시거늘
v.KJV: And saw him saying unto me, Make haste, and get thee quickly out of Jerusalem: for they will not receive thy testimony concerning me.
v.NASB: and I saw Him saying to me, 'Make haste, and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about Me.'
v.NIV: and saw the Lord speaking to me. ‘Quick!’ he said. ‘Leave Jerusalem immediately, because the people here will not accept your testimony about me.’
v.NRSV: and saw Jesus saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
v.SAENEW: 주님이 내게 말씀하시는 것을 보았습니다. 그는 말씀하시기를 '서둘러서 예루살렘을 떠나라. 예루살렘 사람들이 나에 관한 네 증언을 받아들이지 않을 것이기 때문이다' 하셨습니다.
v.WLB: 주께서 말씀하시는 것을 보았습니다. ‘서둘러 즉시 예루살렘을 떠나거라. 이곳 사람들은 네가 나를 증언해도 받아들이지 않을 것이다.’

v.CNV: 我说：‘主啊，他们知道我曾把信你的人监禁起来，又在各会堂拷打他们，
v.ESV: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in one synagogue after another I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
v.GAE: 내가 말하기를 주님 내가 주를 믿는 사람들을 가두고 또 각 회당에서 때리고
v.KJV: And I said, Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
v.NASB: 'And I said, 'Lord, they themselves understand that in one synagogue after another I used to imprison and beat those who believed in You.
v.NIV: “‘Lord,’ I replied, ‘these people know that I went from one synagogue to another to imprison and beat those who believe in you.
v.NRSV: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 내가 말하였습니다. '주님, 내가 주님을 믿는 사람들을 가는 곳마다 회당에서 잡아 가두고 때리고 하던 사실을 사람들이 잘 알고 있습니다.
v.WLB: 내가 대답했습니다. ‘주여, 이 사람들은 내가 여러 회당들을 돌아다니며 주를 믿는 사람들을 감옥에 가두고 때렸다는 것을 압니다.

v.CNV: 并且你的见证人司提反受害流血的时候，我也亲自在场，表示同意，并且为杀他的人看守衣服。’
v.ESV: And when the blood of Stephen your witness was being shed, I myself was standing by and approving and watching over the garments of those who killed him.’
v.GAE: 또 주의 증인 스데반이 피를 흘릴 때에 내가 곁에 서서 찬성하고 그 죽이는 사람들의 옷을 지킨 줄 그들도 아나이다
v.KJV: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
v.NASB: 'And when the blood of Your witness Stephen was being shed, I also was standing by approving, and watching out for the coats of those who were slaying him.'
v.NIV: And when the blood of your martyr Stephen was shed, I stood there giving my approval and guarding the clothes of those who were killing him.’
v.NRSV: And while the blood of your witness Stephen was shed, I myself was standing by, approving and keeping the coats of those who killed him.’
v.SAENEW: 그리고 주님의 증언자인 스데반이 피를 흘리고 죽임을 당할 때에, 나도 곁에 서서, 그 일에 찬동하면서, 그를 죽이는 사람들의 옷을 지키고 있었습니다.'
v.WLB: 그리고 주의 증인 스데반이 피 흘릴 때 나도 그곳에 서서 그 일에 찬성하고 그를 죽이던 사람들의 옷을 지켜 주었습니다.’

v.CNV: 他对我说：‘你走吧，我要派你到远方的外族人那里去。’”
v.ESV: And he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’ ”
v.GAE: 나더러 또 이르시되 떠나가라 내가 너를 멀리 이방인에게로 보내리라 하셨느니라
v.KJV: And he said unto me, Depart: for I will send thee far hence unto the Gentiles.
v.NASB: 'And He said to me, 'Go! For I will send you far away to the Gentiles.''
v.NIV: “Then the Lord said to me, ‘Go; I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’ ”
v.NRSV: Then he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 주님께서 말씀하시기를 '가라. 내가 너를 멀리 이방 사람들에게로 보내겠다' 하셨습니다."
v.WLB: 그러자 주께서 내게 ‘가거라. 내가 너를 저 멀리 이방 사람들에게 보낼 것이다’라고 말씀하셨습니다.”

v.CNV: 众人听见他说到这句话，就高声说：“这样的人应该从地上除掉，不应该活着！”
v.ESV: Up to this word they listened to him. Then they raised their voices and said, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
v.GAE: 이 말하는 것까지 그들이 듣다가 소리 질러 이르되 이러한 자는 세상에서 없애 버리자 살려 둘 자가 아니라 하여
v.KJV: And they gave him audience unto this word, and then lifted up their voices, and said, Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
v.NASB: They listened to him up to this statement, and then they raised their voices and said, 'Away with such a fellow from the earth, for he should not be allowed to live!'
v.NIV: The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they raised their voices and shouted, “Rid the earth of him! He’s not fit to live!”
v.NRSV: Up to this point they listened to him, but then they shouted, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
v.SAENEW: 사람들이 바울의 말을 여기까지 듣고 있다가 "이런 자는 없애 버려라. 살려 두면 안 된다" 하고 소리를 질렀다.
v.WLB: 사람들은 바울의 말을 이 대목까지 듣고 있다가 소리 높여 외쳤습니다. “저 사람을 세상에서 없애 버리자! 저런 놈은 그냥 살려 둘 수 없다!”

v.CNV: 大家正在喊叫着，拋掷衣服，向空中扬灰撒土的时候，
v.ESV: And as they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
v.GAE: 떠들며 옷을 벗어 던지고 티끌을 공중에 날리니
v.KJV: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
v.NASB: And as they were crying out and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust into the air,
v.NIV: As they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
v.NRSV: And while they were shouting, throwing off their cloaks, and tossing dust into the air,
v.SAENEW: 그리고 그들은 고함을 치며, 옷을 벗어 던지며, 공중에 먼지를 날렸다.
v.WLB: 그들이 소리를 지르고 자기 옷을 벗어던지며 공중에 흙을 뿌리자

v.CNV: 千夫长下令把保罗带到营楼去，吩咐人用鞭子拷问他，要知道群众为什么这样向他喊叫。
v.ESV: the tribune ordered him to be brought into the barracks, saying that he should be examined by flogging, to find out why they were shouting against him like this.
v.GAE: 천부장이 바울을 영내로 데려가라 명하고 그들이 무슨 일로 그에 대하여 떠드는지 알고자 하여 채찍질하며 심문하라 한대
v.KJV: The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
v.NASB: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, stating that he should be examined by scourging so that he might find out the reason why they were shouting against him that way.
v.NIV: the commander ordered that Paul be taken into the barracks. He directed that he be flogged and interrogated in order to find out why the people were shouting at him like this.
v.NRSV: the tribune directed that he was to be brought into the barracks, and ordered him to be examined by flogging, to find out the reason for this outcry against him.
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 천부장이 바울을 병영 안으로 끌어들이라고 명령하였다. 그리고 그는 유대 사람들이 바울에게 이렇게 소리를 지르는 이유를 알아내려고, 바울을 채찍질하면서 캐물어 보라고 하였다.
v.WLB: 천부장이 바울을 병영 안으로 들이라고 명령했습니다. 그는 사람들이 왜 이처럼 바울에게 소리를 지르는지 알아내려고 그를 채찍질하고 신문하라고 지시했습니다.

v.CNV: 士兵正用皮带绑他的时候，保罗对站在旁边的百夫长说：“你们鞭打一个还没有定罪的罗马公民，是合法的吗？”
v.ESV: But when they had stretched him out for the whips, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it lawful for you to flog a man who is a Roman citizen and uncondemned?”
v.GAE: 가죽 줄로 바울을 매니 바울이 곁에 서 있는 백부장더러 이르되 너희가 로마 시민 된 자를 죄도 정하지 아니하고 채찍질할 수 있느냐 하니
v.KJV: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said unto the centurion that stood by, Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
v.NASB: But when they stretched him out with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, 'Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman and uncondemned?'
v.NIV: As they stretched him out to flog him, Paul said to the centurion standing there, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who hasn’t even been found guilty?”
v.NRSV: But when they had tied him up with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who is uncondemned?”
v.SAENEW: 그들이 채찍질을 하려고 바울을 눕혔을 때에, 바울은 거기에 서 있는 백부장에게 "로마 시민을 유죄판결도 내리지 않고 매질하는 법이 어디에 있소?" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 바울은 군인들이 자기를 채찍질하려고 묶자 거기 서 있던 백부장에게 말했습니다. “아직 판결을 받지 않은 로마 시민을 채찍질하는 것이 합법한 것입니까?”

v.CNV: 百夫长听了，就去报告千夫长，说：“这个人是罗马公民，你要怎么办呢？”
v.ESV: When the centurion heard this, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman citizen.”
v.GAE: 백부장이 듣고 가서 천부장에게 전하여 이르되 어찌하려 하느냐 이는 로마 시민이라 하니
v.KJV: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the chief captain, saying, Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.
v.NASB: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and told him, saying, 'What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman.'
v.NIV: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. “What are you going to do?” he asked. “This man is a Roman citizen.”
v.NRSV: When the centurion heard that, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.”
v.SAENEW: 백부장이 이 말을 듣고, 천부장에게로 가서 "어떻게 하시렵니까? 이 사람은 로마 시민입니다" 하고 알렸다.
v.WLB: 백부장은 이 말을 듣고 천부장에게 가서 그대로 보고하며 물었습니다. “어떻게 하시렵니까? 이 사람이 로마 시민이랍니다.”

v.CNV: 千夫长就来问保罗：“告诉我，你是罗马公民吗？”他说：“是的。”
v.ESV: So the tribune came and said to him, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
v.GAE: 천부장이 와서 바울에게 말하되 네가 로마 시민이냐 내게 말하라 이르되 그러하다
v.KJV: Then the chief captain came, and said unto him, Tell me, art thou a Roman? He said, Yea.
v.NASB: The commander came and said to him, 'Tell me, are you a Roman?' And he said, 'Yes.'
v.NIV: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes, I am,” he answered.
v.NRSV: The tribune came and asked Paul, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
v.SAENEW: 그러자 천부장이 바울에게로 와서 "내게 말하시오. 당신이 로마 시민이오?" 하고 물었다. 바울이 그렇다고 대답하니,
v.WLB: 천부장이 바울에게 와서 물었습니다. “말해 보시오. 당신이 정말 로마 시민이오?” 바울이 대답했습니다. “그렇소.”

v.CNV: 千夫长说：“我花了一大笔钱，才取得罗马籍。”保罗说：“我生下来就是罗马公民。”
v.ESV: The tribune answered, “I bought this citizenship for a large sum.” Paul said, “But I am a citizen by birth.”
v.GAE: 천부장이 대답하되 나는 돈을 많이 들여 이 시민권을 얻었노라 바울이 이르되 나는 나면서부터라 하니
v.KJV: And the chief captain answered, With a great sum obtained I this freedom. And Paul said, But I was free born.
v.NASB: The commander answered, 'I acquired this citizenship with a large sum of money.' And Paul said, 'But I was actually born a citizen.'
v.NIV: Then the commander said, “I had to pay a lot of money for my citizenship.” “But I was born a citizen,” Paul replied.
v.NRSV: The tribune answered, “It cost me a large sum of money to get my citizenship.” Paul said, “But I was born a citizen.”
v.SAENEW: 천부장은 "나는 돈을 많이 들여서 이 시민권을 얻었소" 하고 말하였다. 바울은 "나는 나면서부터입니다" 하고 말하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 천부장이 말했습니다. “나는 많은 돈을 들여 로마 시민권을 얻었소.” 그러자 바울이 대답했습니다. “나는 태어나면서부터 로마 시민이었소.”

v.CNV: 于是那些要拷问他的人，立刻离开他走了。千夫长既知道他是罗马公民，又因捆绑过他，就害怕起来。
v.ESV: So those who were about to examine him withdrew from him immediately, and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
v.GAE: 심문하려던 사람들이 곧 그에게서 물러가고 천부장도 그가 로마 시민인 줄 알고 또 그 결박한 것 때문에 두려워하니라
v.KJV: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.
v.NASB: Therefore those who were about to examine him immediately let go of him; and the commander also was afraid when he found out that he was a Roman, and because he had put him in chains.
v.NIV: Those who were about to interrogate him withdrew immediately. The commander himself was alarmed when he realized that he had put Paul, a Roman citizen, in chains.
v.NRSV: Immediately those who were about to examine him drew back from him; and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
v.SAENEW: 그러자 바울을 신문하려고 하던 사람들이 곧 물러갔다. 천부장도 바울이 로마 시민이라는 사실을 알고는, 그를 결박해 놓은 일로 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 바울을 신문하려던 사람들이 이 말을 듣고 곧 물러갔습니다. 천부장도 바울이 로마 시민이라는 사실을 알고 그를 결박한 일로 두려워했습니다.

v.CNV: 第二天，千夫长为要知道犹太人控告保罗的真相，就解开他，召集了祭司长和公议会全体在一起，把保罗带下来，叫他站在他们面前。
v.ESV: But on the next day, desiring to know the real reason why he was being accused by the Jews, he unbound him and commanded the chief priests and all the council to meet, and he brought Paul down and set him before them.
v.GAE: 이튿날 천부장은 유대인들이 무슨 일로 그를 고발하는지 진상을 알고자 하여 그 결박을 풀고 명하여 제사장들과 온 공회를 모으고 바울을 데리고 내려가서 그들 앞에 세우니라
v.KJV: On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
v.NASB: But on the next day, wishing to know for certain why he had been accused by the Jews, he released him and ordered the chief priests and all the Council to assemble, and brought Paul down and set him before them.
v.NIV: The commander wanted to find out exactly why Paul was being accused by the Jews. So the next day he released him and ordered the chief priests and all the members of the Sanhedrin to assemble. Then he brought Paul and had him stand before them.
v.NRSV: Since he wanted to find out what Paul was being accused of by the Jews, the next day he released him and ordered the chief priests and the entire council to meet. He brought Paul down and had him stand before them.
v.SAENEW: 이튿날 천부장은 무슨 일로 유대 사람이 바울을 고소하는지, 그 진상을 알아보려고 하였다. 그래서 그는 바울의 결박을 풀어주고, 명령을 내려서, 대제사장들과 온 의회를 모이게 하였다. 그리고 그는 바울을 데리고 내려가서, 그들 앞에 세웠다.
v.WLB: 이튿날 천부장은 바울이 왜 유대 사람들에게 고소를 당했는지 정확히 알아보려고 대제사장들과 온 공회를 소집하고 바울을 풀어서 그들 앞에 데려오게 했습니다.
